title: Point Reyes Light — October 2, 1980 (Page 3)
author: Point Reyes Light Staff
card_summary: A single page from the Point Reyes Light, a weekly community newspaper serving Point Reyes Station, California, dated October 2, 1980, Page 3. This issue was published during the final years of PKD's life, when he was completing works such as VALIS. Issues from this paper are preserved as contextual documents relating to the locales and communities significant to PKD's biography.
page_summary:
A scanned newspaper page from the Point Reyes Light (Point Reyes Station, California), dated Thursday, October 2, 1980, page 3. The clipping was sourced from Newspapers.com and downloaded on April 9, 2025. The PDF text layer contains only source metadata with no recoverable article text.

By 1980, Dick was living in Santa Ana, California, working on The Transmigration of Timothy Archer and continuing his massive Exegesis project. The Point Reyes Light, which had won a Pulitzer Prize in 1979 for its coverage of Synanon, continued to serve the western Marin County community where Dick had once lived. The specific relevance of this clipping to Dick studies remains to be determined through examination of the scanned image.
